zh.title: 比特币/美元技术分析：高位震荡偏强运行
zh.content:
BTC/USD当前维持高位震荡偏强结构，价格围绕76750附近窄幅整理，短线多头仍占优，但动能略有放缓，关注突破方向中运行

技术指标方面，价格站上20日均线76383附近，均线系统略呈多头排列；MACD柱状维持正值，动能偏多，但尚未放量震荡延续

下方支撑关注76380与昨日低点76712下方区间，上方阻力集中在76760-76800附近，突破则打开空间延续中

操作建议以区间交易为主，回踩均线可轻仓试多，若跌破支撑需谨慎；短线关注突破后的顺势跟进机会为宜执行中

en.title: Bitcoin vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Bitcoin is currently in a tight consolidation phase, trading around 76,757 with virtually no 24-hour change. Price action is compressing within a very narrow intraday range, suggesting equilibrium between buyers and sellers. This kind of low-volatility structure often precedes a directional expansion. Despite the lack of momentum, the broader short-term structure remains mildly constructive as price holds above key moving average support.

From an indicator standpoint, the SMA(20) at 76,383 is acting as a dynamic support baseline, with price comfortably positioned above it, reinforcing a modest bullish bias. The MACD reading of 271.68 indicates positive momentum, though the absence of a signal line limits full confirmation of trend strength. RSI is unavailable, but price behavior suggests a neutral-to-slightly-accumulation phase rather than overbought conditions.

Key resistance is located at the recent intraday high near 76,762, which also aligns closely with the current price ceiling. A clean break above this level could open the door toward 77,000, followed by 77,500 as the next psychological extension. On the downside, immediate support sits at 76,712, followed by stronger structural support at the SMA20 around 76,383, with deeper protection near 76,000.

For trading approaches, range strategies remain most effective while price stays compressed between support and resistance. Buying dips toward 76,700–76,400 with tight risk control below the SMA20 may offer favorable risk-reward. Alternatively, breakout traders should wait for a confirmed move above 76,762 with volume expansion before targeting higher levels. Until volatility returns, disciplined position sizing and avoidance of premature breakout entries remain critical for managing whipsaw risk.
